sources: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson: 'we've grown to become very reliant on p cards, and that's been a nightmare for my side of it trying to figure out what people are even buying'
Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: 'we just have a lot of different business units kind of functioning in their own ecosphere'

Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: 'Five separate ERP instances in use, mainly Oracle Purchasing for IT purchase orders' and 'Five PeopleSoft instances handle various non-IT operations without issued POs'
Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: sourcing team furloughed during COVID, 'everyone had found new jobs', team is now 'very, very small' and 'in the hair trying to pick up pieces'
metrics.narrative: Patrick articulated operational pain on the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call: Travel + Leisure has grown highly reliant on P-cards with minimal PO coverage, creating poor spend visibility ('a nightmare for my side of it trying to figure out what people are even buying in the first place'), compounded by five siloed ERP instances (Oracle for IT POs, five PeopleSoft instances for non-IT operations) and fragmented business units operating independently. He stated 'we just have a lot of different business units kind of functioning in their own ecosphere' and noted the company lost its sourcing team during COVID furloughs, leaving procurement understaffed and struggling to reclaim control. No quantified savings target, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, or ROI was stated. The pain is real and articulated but unquantified. Patrick mentioned PwC is leading an ERP consolidation initiative to address the silos, but no timeline or success metrics were shared. Converting this qualitative pain into a quantified business case with the relevant stakeholder is the essential next step to advance the deal.

Next Step (May 2026, ~6 weeks ago): 'Check with Patrick to see if any movement with project. if not will close lead.' — no buyer reply, 86 days silent
OpportunityContactRole: 0 buyer-side contacts; Patrick is not formally linked as a Champion or any role
champion.narrative: Patrick Robertson (Strategic Sourcing Manager) is the only engaged contact and is not a champion. He is an interested end-user and influencer who voluntarily joined the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call and expressed genuine interest in Zycus's AI capabilities, multi-ERP integration, and tail-spend management. However, he explicitly stated he does not control the decision timeline ('I need to figure out timing wise with them '), deferred project approval to his IT counterpart Jeffrey and to finance/accounting leadership ('accounting usually drives this thing'), and described himself as gathering information proactively before a formal evaluation begins ('this is me being proactively trying to get ahead of the curve'). He has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, IT leadership, or finance decision-makers. He has not advocated internally for Zycus, shared internal documents, or driven urgency. Most critically, Patrick has gone completely silent for 86 days since the call despite Ed's May follow-up documented in Next Step. A champion who disappears for three months without reply is not a champion. Patrick may become a coach if the deal restarts, but as of today he is a single point of contact with no demonstrated advocacy, no access to power, and no active engagement. The deal has no champion.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025: no competitor, incumbent, or alternative solution named by Patrick
Salesforce Competitors__c: empty
Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: sister company Wyndham Hotel Group uses Zycus (positive reference, not a competitive threat)
Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: previously worked at Disney with SAP (personal experience, not a current-state vendor)
competition.narrative: No competitor was named or discussed on the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call. Patrick did not mention an incumbent source-to-pay vendor, a competitive shortlist, or any other solution under evaluation. He stated Travel + Leisure uses fragmented ERP instances (Oracle, PeopleSoft) with no unified procurement platform and relies heavily on P-cards, implying current-state tool gaps rather than an entrenched competitor. Patrick referenced that sister company Wyndham Hotel Group (post-spin) is a Zycus customer and that he occasionally exchanges vendor notes with Wyndham contacts, which is a positive reference signal but does not indicate Wyndham is a competitor. He also mentioned he previously worked at Disney, which used SAP, suggesting familiarity with ERP-integrated procurement but offering no insight into Travel + Leisure's current vendor landscape. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty. With 86 days of silence and no forward evaluation process, the competitive picture remains unknown. The primary 'competitor' is inertia and the wait-state tied to the PwC ERP-consolidation project, which has no defined end date. When the deal restarts, discovery must include: Are they evaluating other S2P vendors? Is PwC recommending a preferred platform? Is there an incumbent CLM or sourcing tool to displace?

Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: PwC hired for ERP consolidation, 'they had this pie in the sky goal... dollar signs got a lot larger'
identify_pain.narrative: Patrick clearly articulated operational and visibility pain on the 14 Nov 2025 call. Travel + Leisure has become 'very reliant on p cards' with minimal purchase-order coverage, creating a 'nightmare' for strategic sourcing because Patrick cannot see what is being purchased across the organization. The company operates five siloed ERP instances (one Oracle for IT, five PeopleSoft for non-IT) with fragmented business units 'functioning in their own ecosphere', preventing consolidated spend visibility and category leverage. Patrick owns the pain personally: he is the Strategic Sourcing Manager tasked with regaining control, but his team was gutted during COVID furloughs ('everyone had found new jobs') and is now 'very, very small' and 'in the trenches just trying to figure out how to take some ground back'. He stated the company has hired PwC to consolidate the ERP landscape, but timing is unclear and the consolidation project itself has undergone multiple scope and budget revisions ('they had this pie in the sky goal... they've kind of revised it a few times, and the dollar signs got a lot larger'). The pain is real, personally felt by Patrick, and tied to a broader organizational transformation, but it has not been escalated to executive sponsorship or quantified into a financial business case that would drive urgency and budget allocation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025: no discussion of MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, or contracting
Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c: not populated
No PwC implementation scoping or contracting handoff discussed
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or procurement channel mechanics have been discussed. The 14 Nov 2025 discovery call was a product overview; no MSA, SOW, Order Form, security review, data privacy requirements, or implementation timeline was raised by either party. Patrick did not mention Travel + Leisure's contracting process, legal approval requirements, or procurement policies. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is set in Salesforce, so there is no incumbent contract forcing function. Patrick referenced the ongoing PwC ERP-consolidation engagement but did not describe how Zycus contracting would intersect with that project or whether PwC would play an implementation or advisory role in a Zycus deployment. With the deal cold for 86 days and no forward movement, paper process visibility will only become relevant after buyer re-engagement, EB identification, and a committed evaluation timeline. The immediate need is to restart the relationship and map the decision process, not to draft contract terms.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. Patrick Robertson is a Strategic Sourcing Manager, an end-user and influencer but not the budget authority. On the 14 Nov 2025 call he stated he is gathering vendor options proactively so that 'when accounting, that's usually who drives this thing anyway, comes back saying, what kind of options do we have? This is where I wanna be able to step in'. This explicitly identifies accounting/finance leadership as the actual budget holder, but Patrick did not name them, and no OpportunityContactRole exists for an EB, CFO, VP Finance, or C-level sponsor. Patrick also deferred timing and project approval to 'my counterpart of the IT side... Jeffrey... he's the one that's kind of married to the Pwc people right now', signaling that IT leadership (Jeffrey, last name unknown, no contact record) and the PwC engagement lead may gate the budget and timing decision, not Patrick. Without direct EB engagement and sponsorship, this deal has no commercial pathway and cannot advance from the relevant stakeholder and engaging the CFO or VP Finance who will approve the $100k investment is the immediate priority.

decision_process.narrative: No formal decision process, approval chain, or buying committee has been mapped. Patrick stated on the 14 Nov 2025 call that the process is gated externally by the PwC-led ERP consolidation initiative: 'I need to figure out timing wise with them... when things would be appropriate to start moving forward with these other options'. He deferred project timing and next-step approval to his IT counterpart Jeffrey (remote, expected in town 'next week' from 14 Nov 2025, now 7+ months past, status unknown) who is 'married to the Pwc people'. Patrick also noted that 'accounting usually drives this thing', signaling finance leadership will ultimately control the procurement software decision, but he did not name them or describe their approval threshold. He explicitly stated he is gathering options proactively before a formal process begins ('this is me being proactively trying to get ahead of the curve'). No RFP, vendor shortlist, evaluation committee, or decision timeline has been established. The call sequence shows only one discovery touchpoint (14 Nov 2025) with Patrick; no IT, finance, or executive stakeholders have joined. The deal is stuck waiting on an external PwC project milestone with no defined trigger date or internal champion driving urgency.

sources: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: 'you guys have really jumped in with the AI side'

Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick validated interest in Merlin intake, Anna negotiation agent, AP automation, and supplier onboarding after Ed's demo overview
Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick: 'more of just an overview of what you guys have to offer in that context'
decision_criteria.narrative: Patrick's stated evaluation priorities from the 14 Nov 2025 call center on AI/agentic procurement capability (he opened with 'you guys have really jumped in with the AI side... I believe you were at '), multi-ERP integration flexibility ('do you have any issues with having multiple accounting ?'), tail-spend and P-card spend visibility/control, and supplier onboarding speed. He confirmed interest in Zycus's Merlin intake (conversational UI via the relevant stakeholder for end-user adoption), autonomous negotiation agent (Anna) for tail spend, and AP automation with fraud detection. He validated that Oracle and PeopleSoft compatibility is essential and was reassured by Ed's statement that 'Pwc is a customer... they have multiple accounting systems connected'. However, these are Patrick's technical interests, not a formal RFP or buyer committee decision criteria. No BRD, RFI, shortlist, or weighted scorecard has been shared. Patrick stated he is in information-gathering mode ('more of just an overview of what you guys have to offer') and will not drive a formal evaluation until the PwC ERP-consolidation project provides timing clarity. Without a documented decision framework, executive-sponsored criteria, or a competitive evaluation process, this remains early informal discovery, not structured validation.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields are not populated in Salesforce, so no formal AI appetite score exists. However, the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call provides strong qualitative signal that Travel + Leisure is AI Curious. Patrick proactively raised AI as his primary interest ('you guys have really jumped in with the AI side'), attended procurement/AI conferences in 2025, and validated interest in Merlin and Anna agents. He also noted the broader industry trend ('everything's AI always'). However, this is Patrick's personal interest, not an organizational AI mandate. No executive-level AI strategy, digital transformation initiative, or C-level AI sponsor has been identified. The buyer is curious and receptive but not yet AI Hungry (no top-down mandate or quantified AI-driven business case). Elevating the AI narrative from Patrick's tactical interest to CFO/CTO-level strategic priority is the positioning challenge.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created and qualified same-day 14 Nov 2025 following a discovery call. One booth meeting occurred 15 Sep 2025 (not recorded, likely a conference encounter that seeded the later call). Discovery call 14 Nov 2025 was productive: Patrick articulated pain (P-card reliance, ERP silos, understaffed sourcing team) and expressed interest in Zycus AI agents and multi-ERP integration, but deferred all timing and next steps to internal PwC ERP-consolidation project and to IT counterpart Jeffrey. Ed committed to send slides within the hour; Patrick committed to consult with Jeffrey and PwC team for timing. No follow-on activity logged in Salesforce after 14 Nov 2025 except LastActivityDate 1 April 2026 (unknown task/event, 86 days ago). Next Step updated in May 2026 (~6 weeks ago) documents rep outreach: 'Check with Patrick to see if any movement with project. if not will close lead.' No buyer reply received. Deal has been silent and cold for 86 days. No stage progression, no new contacts added, no follow-up calls, no internal champion activity, no PwC or IT engagement. The deal is effectively dormant, waiting on an external milestone (PwC project timing) with no active buyer engagement or internal advocate driving it forward.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Next Step May 2026 (no buyer reply), deal pulse 26 Jun 2026 (86 days silent)
champion_strength.summary: No champion. Patrick Robertson is an interested influencer who voluntarily joined the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call and expressed genuine interest in Zycus AI capabilities and multi-ERP integration, but he has no decision authority, has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer or IT decision-maker, and has been completely silent for 86 days with no reply to the rep's May outreach. A contact who disappears for three months is not a champion. Patrick may re-engage if the PwC project reaches a milestone or if finance leadership initiates a procurement platform evaluation, but as of today he is inactive and has demonstrated no advocacy.

items.action: Re-engage Patrick Robertson directly via phone and email with a binary requalification question.
items.trigger: 86 days silent with no buyer reply to May outreach; deal cold since 1 April 2026; Next Step states 'if not will close lead'.
items.expected_effect: Requalify or disqualify within 7 days. If Patrick confirms active timeline and EB access, restart discovery. If no reply or no committed path forward, close as 'timing unclear, no sponsor' and reallocate resources. Prevents continued investment in a dormant, single-threaded deal with no buyer engagement.

items.action: Secure introduction to IT decision-maker Jeffrey and finance/accounting leadership who control budget and project timing.
items.trigger: Patrick stated 'I need to get with my counterpart of the IT side... Jeffrey... he's the one that's kind of married to the Pwc people' and 'accounting usually drives this thing' on 14 Nov 2025; no IT or finance contact engaged in 7+ months.
items.expected_effect: Break single-thread risk and map true decision authority. Jeffrey controls PwC relationship and project timing; finance leadership controls budget. Without direct access to both, deal cannot advance. Request Patrick (if he re-engages) to facilitate introductions, or use Wyndham Hotel Group customer reference (Stephanie, Robert, Mary Asorio, David Gut) to open side-door into Travel + Leisure finance/IT via shared corporate history.

items.action: Co-develop a quantified business case with finance leadership anchored to tail-spend savings, P-card leakage reduction, and supplier onboarding cycle-time improvement.
items.trigger: Patrick articulated qualitative pain (P-card nightmare, ERP silos, understaffed sourcing team) but no quantified ROI or savings target on 14 Nov 2025; no CFO sponsorship or business case developed in 7+ months.
items.expected_effect: Convert qualitative pain into CFO-level ROI to unlock executive sponsorship and budget allocation. Target: model 3-5% tail-spend savings (conservative Zycus benchmark), 50% reduction in supplier onboarding time (Patrick stated 'our process has been broken'), and X% improvement in spend visibility (addressing P-card leakage). A quantified business case creates urgency and breaks the PwC wait-state by positioning Zycus as the post-consolidation procurement layer that delivers ROI regardless of ERP timing.

items.action: Leverage Wyndham Hotel Group customer reference to accelerate trust and open multi-threaded access into Travel + Leisure.
items.trigger: Patrick stated sister company Wyndham Hotel Group (post-spin from the relevant stakeholder) uses Zycus and that he occasionally exchanges vendor notes with Wyndham contacts on 14 Nov 2025; Wyndham customer contacts confirmed as Stephanie, Robert, Mary Asorio, David Gut.
items.expected_effect: Activate warm peer reference to build credibility and create side-door access to Travel + Leisure finance/IT stakeholders. Coordinate Wyndham-to-Travel+Leisure reference call where Stephanie or David shares Zycus ROI results and multi-ERP integration experience (directly relevant to Patrick's pain). Use reference call to request Wyndham advocate introduce Zycus to Travel + Leisure CFO or CTO, bypassing Patrick's single-thread bottleneck.

items.action: If no buyer re-engagement within 10 business days of rank-1 outreach, disqualify and close opportunity as 'timing unclear, no active sponsor, external dependency unresolved'.
items.trigger: Deal has been cold for 86 days; rep already attempted May outreach with no reply; Next Step explicitly states 'if not will close lead'; no EB, no champion, no forward activity.
items.expected_effect: Prevent continued drag on pipeline forecast and free resources for live opportunities. A deal that is 224 days old, single-threaded to a silent contact, gated by an undefined external project, with no EB or champion, and unresponsive to outreach for 86 days is not a qualified opportunity. Honest disqualification is better than false pipeline. Document lessons learned (need EB access before stage=Qualified, need committed timeline before investment) and reallocate deal-team capacity.

confidence_signals.summary: All confidence signals are negative. Patrick has been unresponsive for 86 days despite May rep outreach. The deal is single-threaded to a non-decision-maker with no EB, no IT engagement, and no champion advocacy. The buyer deferred all timing to an external PwC project with no defined milestone. No quantified business case, no executive sponsorship, no forward activity. The relationship is cold and the deal is at high risk of permanent stall or disqualification.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 14 Nov 2025, qualified same day. 224 days in Qualified stage with no forward movement. Last verified buyer activity 1 April 2026 (86 days ago). Close date 14 Nov 2026 is 141 days out. A Qualified-stage deal 141 days from close with zero buyer engagement for 86 days and no pathway to validation/negotiation is forced Off Track. The rep's May outreach (documented in Next Step: 'Check with Patrick to see if any movement with project. if not will close lead.') received no buyer reply, confirming the relationship is cold.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal frozen 86 days with no buyer response to rep's May outreach. Qualified stage, 141 days from close, single-threaded to Patrick who is waiting on undefined ERP timing. No champion, no EB, no business case, no forward movement in 7+.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is off track and at high risk of disqualification. It has been cold for 86 days with no buyer engagement, remains single-threaded to a non-decision-maker, and is parked in Qualified stage 141 days from close with no pathway to validation. The highest-leverage action is to re-engage Patrick directly, requalify the opportunity against current timing and budget reality, and either secure a committed path forward (including EB access and a near-term next step) or disqualify and reallocate resources.

competitive_position.summary: No direct competitor identified. The primary obstacle is not a rival vendor but internal inertia and the wait-state tied to Travel + Leisure's ongoing PwC-led ERP consolidation initiative, which has an undefined timeline and has undergone multiple scope/budget revisions. Patrick stated on 14 Nov 2025 that he is gathering vendor options proactively before accounting/finance leadership initiates a formal procurement software evaluation, meaning the deal is pre-RFP and pre-shortlist. The 'do nothing' scenario (continuing with fragmented ERPs, P-card reliance, and no unified procurement platform) is the current state and the de facto competitor until executive sponsorship and a quantified business case create urgency. Sister company Wyndham Hotel Group's use of Zycus is a positive reference point but not a competitive factor. When the deal restarts, competitive discovery must confirm: Is PwC recommending a preferred S2P platform? Are other vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer) being evaluated? Is there an incumbent CLM or sourcing tool to displace?

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
competitors.quote: I need to figure out timing wise with them... when things would be appropriate to start moving forward with these other options... I'd rather not reinvent the wheel twice. Just we're gonna pull off the Band Aid, just get everything fixed at the same time or at least best we can.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of delay: P-card leakage, missed tail-spend savings, manual sourcing inefficiency, and compliance risk while waiting for PwC project completion. Position Zycus as the post-consolidation procurement layer that works across ANY ERP landscape (Oracle, PeopleSoft, SAP) and can deploy in parallel with PwC's work. Secure EB sponsorship to fund the business case and break the wait-state.

items.grounding_quote: I need to get with my counterpart of the IT side... Jeffrey. He is... in town, I think, next week... He's the one that's kind of married to the Pwc people right now... I need to figure out timing wise with them.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong at influencer level, unproven at executive level
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was the centerpiece of the 14 Nov 2025 discovery call and resonated strongly with Patrick. He opened the conversation by stating 'you guys have really jumped in with the AI side' and noted he had just returned from an Amazon conference 'and everything's AI always', signaling high receptivity to AI-led procurement messaging. Ed positioned Zycus as a leader in agentic AI, highlighting Merlin intake (conversational UI via MS Teams for end-user adoption) and Anna (autonomous negotiation agent for tail spend), both of which directly address Patrick's stated pain points (P-card visibility, tail-spend control). Patrick validated the positioning multiple times during the call. However, the AI narrative has not reached executive stakeholders (EB, CFO, IT decision-maker Jeffrey) who would fund and sponsor the transformation. The positioning is strong at the influencer level but untested and un-landed at the power level.

items.quote: You guys have really jumped in with the AI side... I believe you were there....everything's AI always.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson
items.criterion: AI-driven procurement (Merlin intake conversational UI, Anna autonomous tail-spend negotiation)

items.quote: Do you have any issues with having multiple accounting? ...Pwc is a customer... they have multiple accounting systems connected. ...we can integrate to multiples. ...Gotcha. Okay. Makes sense.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson and Ed Dlugosz

items.quote: We've grown to become very reliant on p cards, and that's been a nightmare for my side of it trying to figure out what people are even buying....Anna for your tailspin....tail savings, usually between two and 3, honestly I see companies going between 4-10% savings.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson and Ed Dlugosz
items.criterion: Tail-spend visibility and control (to combat P-card reliance and missing PO coverage)

items.quote: Any ideas on how long it's taken you to onboard a supplier right now? ...our process has been broken for a little while. ...BDO Unibank, 99.5% invoice compliance, supplier onboarding down to 8 days.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson and Ed Dlugosz

items.quote: I need to figure out timing wise with them... when things would be appropriate to start moving forward....I'd rather not reinvent the wheel twice....Just we're gonna pull off the Band Aid, just get everything fixed at the same time.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson
items.criterion: Timing alignment with PwC ERP-consolidation project

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Patrick's stated needs from the 14 Nov 2025 call align well with Zycus ANA capabilities: AI-driven procurement (Merlin intake, Anna negotiation agent), multi-ERP integration flexibility (critical given five instances of Oracle and PeopleSoft), tail-spend visibility and control (to combat P-card reliance), and supplier onboarding automation. Ed addressed each requirement during the discovery call, citing PwC as a Zycus customer running multiple accounting systems and highlighting Zycus's out-of-box ERP adapters, autonomous tail-spend negotiation, and conversational UI via MS Teams. Patrick validated the fit multiple times ('Sounds good', 'Gotcha. Okay. Makes sense', 'Yeah. I think so '). However, this is product-level fit based on Patrick's technical interests, not executive-validated strategic fit. The broader organizational fit question is unanswered: Does Travel + Leisure's finance/accounting leadership (the budget holders) prioritize procurement transformation now, or will it remain subordinate to the PwC ERP project for 6-12+ months? Without EB engagement and a quantified business case, product fit alone cannot advance the deal.

hard.ais_why:
• The opportunity itself is named around ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent) — Edward Dlugosz explicitly positioned this deal on agentic AI.
• May next-step reads as stalled: rep planning to check with Patrick and close the lead if there's no movement — the AI hook is cooling.
• Created November 2025 with last activity 4/1/26 — an ANA-framed opp now in stall mode pending the prospect's response.
hard.next_step: May - Hi Patrick, Check with Patrick to see if any movement with project. if not will close lead.

pulse.summary: Live: buyer call(s) read this sweep.

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: Re-engage or disqualify: 86 days cold with no buyer reply to May outreach. Patrick deferred all timing to an external PwC project with no defined milestone. Direct outreach to Patrick (phone + email) with a binary forcing question is the immediate action: Is there a committed timeline and budget for procurement transformation in 2026, or should this opportunity be closed? If Patrick does not reply within 10 business days, disqualify and close as 'timing unclear, no active sponsor'.

Single-threaded risk on a non-decision-maker: Patrick Robertson is an end-user influencer, not the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker. He explicitly stated 'accounting usually drives this thing' and deferred project timing to IT counterpart Jeffrey and to the relevant stakeholder 0 OpportunityContactRoles and no IT, finance, or executive engagement in 7+ months, this deal has no power coverage and no internal champion. Multi-threading to the CFO/VP Finance (budget authority) and to Jeffrey (IT gatekeeper) is essential before any discovery or demo investment.

value.name: Do Nothing / Status Quo
value.quote: I need to figure out timing wise with them... when things would be appropriate to start moving forward with these other options... I'd rather not reinvent the wheel twice. Just we're gonna pull off the Band Aid, just get everything fixed at the same time or at least best we can.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 14 Nov 2025, Patrick Robertson
value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of delay: P-card leakage, missed tail-spend savings, manual sourcing inefficiency, and compliance risk while waiting for PwC project completion. Position Zycus as the post-consolidation procurement layer that works across ANY ERP landscape (Oracle, PeopleSoft, SAP) and can deploy in parallel with PwC's work. Secure EB sponsorship to fund the business case and break the wait-state.
